beginner.note: Grammar mistakes are noted but weighted less heavily

templates.title: Journal Entry - Best Day Recently
templates.prompt:
Write a diary/journal entry about the best day you had recently. Describe:
- What happened that day
- Who you were with
- How you felt
- Why it was special to you

Start with: 'Dear Diary,' and write in first person (I, my, me).
llm_checks: Capital letters at start of sentences

templates.title: Dialogue - Patient at the Doctor
templates.prompt:
Complete this conversation. Write ONLY the customer's responses (marked [YOU]):

Doctor: Good morning! How are you feeling today?
You: [YOUR RESPONSE]

Doctor: I see. How long have you had this problem?
You: [YOUR RESPONSE]

Doctor: Do you have any allergies to medicine?
You: [YOUR RESPONSE]

Doctor: I will prescribe some medicine. Take it twice a day. Understood?
You: [YOUR RESPONSE]

Make your responses natural and polite.

templates.title: Short Message - Cancelling Plans
templates.prompt:
Write a short message to your friend explaining that you cannot meet them today as planned. Include:
- Why you cannot come
- How you feel about missing it
- When you can meet instead
- A friendly ending

Keep it casual - this is to a friend.

templates.title: Simple Email to Teacher - Absent from Class
templates.prompt:
Write an email to your teacher explaining why you missed class yesterday.

Include:
- A subject line
- A greeting
- The reason you were absent
- An apology
- Ask for any missed work
- A polite ending with your name

You were absent because you were sick with a fever.

templates.title: Describing Your Favourite Room
templates.prompt:
Describe your bedroom or your favourite room in your home.

Write about:
- What the room looks like
- What is in the room
- What colours you see
- Why you like this room
- What you do in this room

Use describing words (adjectives) to make it interesting.

templates.title: Professional Email - Day Off Request
templates.prompt:
Write a professional email to your manager requesting one day off next Friday for a medical appointment.

Your email must include:
- A clear subject line
- Proper formal greeting
- Clear reason for the request
- Assurance that work will not suffer
- Polite request for approval
- Professional sign-off

Remember: This is to your manager, so keep it professional and respectful.

templates.title: Formal Complaint Letter - Restaurant
templates.prompt:
Write a formal complaint letter to the manager of a restaurant where you had a disappointing experience last Saturday.

The problems were:
- You waited 45 minutes for your food
- The food arrived cold
- The waiter was rude when you complained
- You were charged for a dish you didn't order

Include:
- Your contact details (make them up)
- Date
- Formal greeting
- Clear explanation of each problem
- What you want them to do about it
- Polite but firm closing

templates.title: Opinion Essay - Social Media
templates.prompt:
Write a short essay giving YOUR OPINION on:

'Social media does more harm than good for young people.'

Your essay MUST have:
- Introduction (state your opinion clearly)
- Paragraph 1 (first reason/argument)
- Paragraph 2 (second reason/argument)
- Paragraph 3 (opposing view + your counter)
- Conclusion (restate opinion, final thought)

Use transition words between paragraphs:
(However, Furthermore, In conclusion, etc.)

templates.title: Story Continuation - The Mysterious Letter
templates.prompt:
Continue this story for at least 3 more paragraphs. Keep the same tone and style:

--- START OF STORY ---
'The letter arrived on a Tuesday morning, which was strange because the post never came before noon. Sarah picked it up from the doormat and noticed there was no stamp, no return address — only her name written in handwriting she did not recognize.'
--- END OF STORY ---

Your continuation must:
- Make logical sense with the beginning
- Have a clear sequence of events
- Use descriptive language
- Include at least one piece of dialogue
- End at an interesting point (not THE end)

templates.title: Meeting Summary - Marketing Campaign
templates.prompt:
Read this information and write a clear meeting summary:

INFORMATION:
- Date: Monday, January 20, 2025
- Attendees: Sarah (Manager), Tom (Sales), Lisa (Marketing), David (Finance)
- Topic 1: Sales dropped 15% last quarter
- Topic 2: New marketing campaign planned for February - social media focus
- Topic 3: Budget approved for campaign: $5,000
- Decision: Launch campaign on February 1st
- Action: Tom to prepare sales report by Friday. Lisa to design social media posts.
- Next meeting: February 3rd, 2 PM

Write this as a professional meeting summary with clear headings and bullet points where appropriate.

templates.title: Formal Report - Four-Day Working Week
templates.prompt:
Write a formal report for your company manager on the topic:

'The Benefits and Challenges of Introducing a Four-Day Working Week'

Your report MUST follow this structure:
1. Title
2. Introduction (purpose of report)
3. Benefits (at least 3 points)
4. Challenges (at least 2 points)
5. Recommendation (what you suggest)
6. Conclusion

Use formal language throughout.
Use headings for each section.
Write in third person where possible (avoid 'I think' - use 'It is recommended')

templates.title: News Article - School Wins Robotics Competition
templates.prompt:
Write a short newspaper article using this information:

FACTS:
- City: Manchester, England
- Event: Local school wins national robotics competition
- School: Greenfield Secondary School
- Students: Team of 5, aged 14-16
- Competition: National STEM Challenge 2025
- Their robot: Could sort recycling automatically
- Teacher: Mr. Ahmed Khan led the team
- Prize: £10,000 and a trip to NASA

Your article MUST include:
- A strong headline
- Opening paragraph (who, what, where, when)
- Quote from a student (make it up)
- Quote from the teacher
- Background information
- Closing statement

templates.title: Cover Letter - Junior Marketing Assistant
templates.prompt:
Write a cover letter for this job:

JOB AD:
Position: Junior Marketing Assistant
Company: BrightMedia Agency
Requirements:
- Degree in Marketing or related field
- Good communication skills
- Social media experience
- Ability to work in a team
- Creative thinking

You are applying. Make up:
- Your name and contact details
- Your relevant qualification
- 2-3 relevant experiences or skills
- Why you want THIS specific company

Your letter MUST have:
- Your address and date
- Company address
- Formal greeting (Dear Hiring Manager)
- Opening: why you are writing
- Body: your relevant skills/experience
- Why you want this job/company specifically
- Closing: request for interview
- Formal sign-off

templates.title: Argumentative Essay - Artificial Intelligence & Jobs
templates.prompt:
Write a well-structured argumentative essay on this topic:

'Artificial Intelligence will create more jobs than it destroys.'

Do you AGREE or DISAGREE?

REQUIRED STRUCTURE:
Para 1 - Introduction: Hook sentence + background + thesis statement
Para 2 - Argument FOR (even if you disagree, present the strongest case for this view)
Para 3 - Argument AGAINST
Para 4 - Your analysis: which side is stronger and why, with specific examples
Para 5 - Conclusion: Restate thesis, summary, final thought

REQUIREMENTS:
- Use academic vocabulary
- Use at least 4 different transition words
- Include at least 2 specific examples
- No contractions
- Varied sentence structures
- Clear logical progression
